classes.title: Muay Thai
classes.subtitle: National sport of Thailand — the art of eight limbs.
classes.description: Sharp kicks, ruthless clinch chains, elbows, and fight IQ under fatigue. Built on bags, pads, and honest sparring progressions.
classes.overview: Muay Thai at Afara Top Team emphasizes technical striking, cardio that carries into real exchanges, and a culture of respect inside hard rounds. Coaches ladder you from novice footwork through ring-style pacing.

classes.title: Boxing
classes.subtitle: Pure hands — footwork, head movement, pacing.
classes.description: Classic sweet science sharpened with modern athletics: defense-first fundamentals, combos that travel, and power you can steer.
classes.overview: Boxers here spend real time on the basics that win rounds: slips, exits, tempo changes, and clean straight lines. Bags, mitts, and controlled technical sparring reinforce what you drill.

classes.title: MMA
classes.subtitle: Blend striking, wrestling, and finishing instincts.
classes.description: Position-to-position rounds that force real transitions: cage craft, scramble defense, top pressure, and composure.
classes.overview: MMA classes splice stand-up shells with wrestling entries and ground-and-pound awareness. Sparring ramps are coached to role — no chaos without context.

classes.title: Wrestling
classes.subtitle: Takedowns, rides, chains — dictate where the fight lives.
classes.description: From collar ties to relentless leg attacks: learn to impose ties, disrupt balance, and own the scramble.
classes.overview: Wrestling blocks feed both MMA and grappling pursuits. Afara Top Team builds chain wrestling and defensive instincts so exchanges don’t evaporate.

coaches.name: Dana Ortega
coaches.bio: NCAA-qualified athlete turned coach. Builds chain wrestling curricula for beginners through competitive adults.

coaches.name: Marisol Vek
coaches.bio: Stadium-style pad holder and clinician. Passionate about clean mechanics and fearless clinch reps.

coaches.name: Jax Pollard
coaches.bio: Former regional amateur champion emphasizing defense-first pacing and repeatable power lanes.

coaches.name: River Cheng
coaches.bio: Blur the lines responsibly — transitions, cage geography, and composure drills for hybrid athletes.

fighters.name: MOHAMAD HASSON
fighters.note: Pro debut prep — Afara Top Team striking lab

locationsSection.heading: Weekly schedule
locationsSection.description: Pick your bay — each location runs the same coaching standard with a localized timetable.

vision.heading: Our vision
vision.lead: Fighters train to be strong — we channel that strength back into the communities that raised us.
vision.extendedPageIntro: Afara Top Team's NGO work sits alongside our academies: food drives, youth mentorship, disaster relief, and long-term community partnerships across Lebanon. The dojo builds discipline; the outreach builds trust.

hero.headline: Strength in the gym. Service in the streets.
hero.subheadline: Our humanitarian arm turns athlete energy into real community impact — from Saida to Ketermaya and every village in between.

vision.pillarsSectionLead: Six commitments that guide every outreach program we run.

homePreview.headline: Community impact built on the mat

pillars.title: Community aid
pillars.description: Food parcels, winter supplies, and essentials delivered directly to families who need them most.

pillars.title: Youth outreach
pillars.description: Free mat days, mentorship, and discipline programs that give kids structure outside the ring.

pillars.title: Disaster response
pillars.description: Rapid mobilization when crises hit — volunteers, supplies, and on-the-ground coordination.

pillars.title: Lasting impact
pillars.description: Partnerships with local leaders so aid isn't a one-off — it's a relationship we maintain.

pillars.title: Volunteer corps
pillars.description: Fighters, coaches, and families who show up on weekends — packing, driving, and lifting where it counts.

pillars.title: Local partners
pillars.description: Municipalities, NGOs, and community leaders who help us reach the right people at the right time.

mission.heading: Our mission
mission.lead: Three pillars of action — every event documented, every hand counted.

cards.title: Show up first
cards.description: When a community asks for help, our teams mobilize before the cameras arrive. Coaches, fighters, and volunteers on the ground within days.

cards.title: Train to serve
cards.description: Members contribute through volunteer hours, fundraising drives, and skills — logistics, medical support, youth coaching, and more.

cards.title: Stay accountable
cards.description: Every humanitarian event is logged, photographed, and shared transparently. Donors and partners see exactly where resources go.

stats.label: Events held
stats.detail: Humanitarian drives and community programs since launch.

stats.label: Families reached
stats.detail: Aid parcels, meals, and supplies delivered across all branches.

stats.label: Volunteers
stats.detail: Coaches, fighters, and members who show up for outreach.

eventsSection.heading: Humanitarian events
eventsSection.lead: Real work, real communities — stories from our outreach programs.

humanitarianEvents.title: Winter aid drive — Saida
humanitarianEvents.excerpt: 200+ winter supply kits distributed to families across the coastal district, with hot meals served on site.

humanitarianEvents.title: Free youth mat day — Barja
humanitarianEvents.excerpt: Open gym day for 60+ kids — intro to boxing and wrestling, plus mentorship talks from our coaching staff.

humanitarianEvents.title: Flood relief — Kfarhata
humanitarianEvents.excerpt: Emergency supply run after seasonal flooding — water, blankets, and cleanup crews deployed within 48 hours.

humanitarianEvents.title: Ramadan meal packs — Ketermaya
humanitarianEvents.excerpt: 500 iftar meal packs prepared and delivered in partnership with local community leaders.

humanitarianEvents.title: Back-to-school supplies — Saida
humanitarianEvents.excerpt: Backpacks, notebooks, and uniforms for 150 students ahead of the new school year.

humanitarianEvents.title: Community health day — Barja
humanitarianEvents.excerpt: Free basic health screenings and fitness assessments for residents, run by volunteer nurses and coaches.

about.lead: Benefits that compound — not spreadsheets. Stress relief, structured skill growth, and a crew that holds you accountable.
about.goalsHeadline: Make elite coaching accessible. Keep standards high. Leave ego at the door.
about.goalsBody: We build capable humans first — competition paths are optional. Every program ladders skills clearly so you always know what you're training toward.
about.benefitsLead: How training tends to surface in daily member check-ins.
about.statCoachingText: Black-belt and pro-level instructors across striking & grappling.
about.statMembersText: Training sessions logged across all Afara Top Team locations last season.
about.extendedPageIntro: Afara Top Team trains strikers, wrestlers, and hybrid athletes across Saida, Barja, Kfarhata, and Ketermaya. Coaches publish clear expectations, hygiene standards stay non-negotiable, and newcomers get a onboarding path regardless of athletic background.

home.classesSubtitle: Full-bleed intensity. Tap a program — each one has depth on its own page.
